Our Philosophy

The Way of Tao

"The Tao that can be told is not the eternal Tao" — Lao Tzu

☯

Harmony

Living in balance with the natural order, aligning body, mind, and spirit with the universal flow of existence.

🪷

Compassion

Cultivating love and kindness toward all beings — the foundation of spiritual life and community service.

✨

Wisdom

Seeking truth through practice, meditation, and the teachings of realized masters who have walked the path before us.

Our Story

About TAN

History, Mission, Vision & Philosophy

HISTORY

Our Journey

The TAO Association of Nepal (TAN) was established in 1996 (2055/056 B.S.) with the vision of spreading Taoist philosophy and spiritual practice in Nepal. Registered under CDORN: 87/055/056 and SWC RN: 26328, TAN has grown from a small group of devoted practitioners into a nationwide organization.

Headquartered in Sitapaila, Kathmandu, TAN operates branches across Nepal including Pokhara, Chitwan, Bhairahawa, and many more — each serving as a center for community, worship, and learning.

Over the decades, TAN has organized hundreds of seminars, constructed numerous temples, and facilitated thousands of practitioners on their spiritual journey.

🎯

Our Mission

To preserve, propagate, and practice the ancient wisdom of Tao in Nepal — nurturing spiritual growth, moral development, and harmonious community life rooted in the Taoist principles of balance, compassion, and virtue.

🌟

Our Vision

A spiritually awakened Nepal where individuals live in harmony with the Tao — contributing to peace, well-being, and enlightenment for all beings, guided by the eternal principles passed down through generations of Taoist masters.

PHILOSOPHY

The Philosophy of Tao

01

Wu Wei — Non-Action

The art of effortless action — acting in accordance with the natural flow of the universe without force or resistance.

02

Yin & Yang — Balance

The interplay of opposing yet complementary forces that create harmony and maintain the dynamic balance of existence.

03

Te — Virtue & Power

Living with authentic virtue — the natural expression of spiritual cultivation that arises from aligning with the Tao.

Spiritual Travel Programs

Sacred journeys to Thailand and beyond

MAY 1–7, 2025

Thailand Tao Temple Pilgrimage

Our flagship annual pilgrimage to the sacred Tao temples of Thailand. Meet revered gurus, participate in traditional ceremonies, and deepen your spiritual practice in an authentic Tao environment.

✈Destination: Bangkok & Chiang Mai, Thailand
📅Duration: 7 Days / 6 Nights
👥Group Size: 20–30 persons
💰Estimated Cost: NPR 65,000 – 80,000
Register for This Journey

OCTOBER 2025

Taiwan Sacred Temple Tour

A profound journey to the spiritual heartland of Taoism in Taiwan, visiting ancient temples, meeting the highest masters, and experiencing the full depth of Taoist religious tradition.

✈Destination: Taipei & Tainan, Taiwan
📅Duration: 10 Days / 9 Nights
👥Group Size: 15–25 persons
💰Estimated Cost: NPR 1,10,000 – 1,30,000
Register for This Journey

Gurus & Masters

Our spiritual teachers and guides

🌟

Revered Master Chen

Supreme Grand Master
Taiwan / Kathmandu

With over 40 years of Tao practice, Master Chen established TAN's connection with the source traditions in Taiwan. He visits Nepal annually to guide practitioners and oversee major ceremonies.

"The Tao gives birth to all things. Return to the Tao, and you will find peace beyond understanding."
☯

Master Li Wei

Senior Spiritual Teacher
Chiang Mai / Pokhara

Master Li oversees the Pokhara branch and leads the annual Thailand pilgrimage. His teachings focus on Wu Wei and the integration of Tao philosophy in daily life.

"Act without action. Accomplish without striving. This is the way of the sage."
🙏

Guru Dharma Shrestha

Nepal Tao Teacher
Kathmandu

The founding spiritual teacher of TAN, Guru Dharma has spent three decades spreading Tao teachings in Nepal, translating ancient texts into Nepali and developing the local practice community.

"Like water, be soft enough to nourish all things, yet strong enough to wear away stone."
🪷

Sister Anita Gurung

Women's Practice Leader
Bhaktapur / Kathmandu

Sister Anita leads the women's spiritual development programs and youth teachings at TAN. She is devoted to making Tao accessible to all members of society.

"The journey of a thousand miles begins with a single step. Take that step with love in your heart."

News & Blog

Articles, updates, and insights

PHILOSOPHY March 10, 2025

Understanding Wu Wei: The Art of Effortless Action

Wu Wei, often translated as 'non-action' or 'effortless action,' is one of the most profound and misunderstood concepts in Tao philosophy. Far from advocating passivity, Wu Wei teaches us to act in perfect alignment with the natural flow of existence...

READ MORE →
TEMPLE UPDATE February 28, 2025

Pokhara Temple Construction: Final Stage Update

We are delighted to announce that the Pokhara Temple construction has entered its final stage, with interior fittings, altar installations, and landscaping now underway. The inauguration ceremony is planned for June 2025...

READ MORE →
TRAVEL January 15, 2025

Thailand Pilgrimage 2024: A Journey of Transformation

Thirty TAN members embarked on last year's Thailand pilgrimage — a journey that transformed hearts and deepened practice. From the ancient temples of Bangkok to the mountain monasteries of Chiang Mai, every moment was filled with grace...

READ MORE →
